overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to disinformation, computational social science, and coordinated inauthentic behavior. LinkedIn boasts the largest following, while Bluesky demonstrates strong engagement through reposts. His content revolves around research publications, conference presentations, and job/research opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Posts related to the study of disinformation, information operations, and coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms.
Content focused on computational methods for analyzing social media data, including algorithm development and applications in social science research.
Posts advertising job openings (postdoctoral positions) and research opportunities (PhD students) in the field of computational social science.

recommendations: Increase cross-promotion of content across platforms to leverage the different audience sizes.
Experiment with visual content (images, videos) to enhance engagement, especially on platforms like LinkedIn and Bluesky.
Actively engage with comments and replies to foster a stronger sense of community.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows a good balance of likes and reposts, suggesting a highly engaged audience. Focus on timely research publications and calls for research.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon audience is engaged in discussions about methodologies and algorithms. Continue sharing technical updates and engaging with relevant conversations.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest follower base and high like rate, making it ideal for professional announcements such as grant awards and job postings. Encourage comments and discussions.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
